projects.summary: Critical transmission infrastructure for the Hunter-Central Coast REZ. Includes new 500kV and 330kV transmission lines and substations. Early works and procurement for major packages are ongoing, with specific tenders for HV components (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) and associated services expected or active. Transgrid is the Network Operator.

World's largest utility-scale BESS for system strength. While main EPC awarded, specialist HV connection equipment (transformers, switchgear for grid connection) and commissioning QAQC/FAT for BESS modules and grid interface components will be crucial and potentially still open or imminent for sub-contractors, with energisation targeted for mid-2025.

Key transmission lines and substations to connect renewable energy projects in the Central-West Orana REZ. While main EPC is underway, specialist HV equipment FAT, installation QAQC, and commissioning for substations (transformers, switchgear, GIS) and transmission lines are ongoing throughout the construction phase, expected completion 2028.

HVDC interconnector project. Procurement is active for major equipment packages, including HVDC converter stations, cables, and associated HV switchgear and transformers. Specialist FAT and QAQC for these critical HV components will be essential and are actively being sought or planned for the upcoming phases. Final Investment Decision expected 2024.

Massive integrated wind, solar, and green hydrogen project. Federal environmental approval secured in March 2024. While early stage, its scale implies significant future procurement for turbines, solar arrays, electrolysers, and extensive HV transmission and substation infrastructure, including transformers and switchgear.

219MW / 877MWh BESS project under construction, targeting operation in early 2025. While civil works and main battery installation are progressing, the grid connection and energisation phase will require significant QAQC and FAT for HV transformers, circuit breakers, and associated switchgear to connect to Western Power's network.

Total Eren is advancing plans for Stage 2 of Kiamal Solar Farm, including a co-located BESS. Procurement for major equipment, including solar panels, inverters, batteries, and the necessary HV substation equipment (transformers, switchgear) for grid connection, is expected to commence within the next 12-18 months. Early works approval granted February 2024.

Neoen is planning a 400MW Stage 2 for the Clarke Creek Wind Farm. Following the successful Stage 1, this expansion will involve significant procurement of turbines, as well as expansion of the existing substation or new HV substation equipment (transformers, switchgear) and transmission lines to integrate the additional capacity. Procurement is anticipated in 2025.

One of the largest onshore wind farms in Australia. Construction is progressing, with turbine installation ongoing. Significant QAQC and FAT requirements remain for the 330kV transmission line, major substations (including HV transformers, GIS, circuit breakers), and switchgear for connection to the grid, with progressive energisation phases.

ACEN is planning expansion of the New England Solar Farm with Stages 2 & 3, including up to 800MW solar and a co-located BESS. Stage 1 is nearing completion. Procurement for major components, including solar arrays, inverters, batteries, and the expansion or upgrade of HV substation equipment (transformers, switchgear) for grid connection, is imminent or expected.

Stanwell is developing a 1GW/2GWh BESS. In March 2024, Stanwell initiated procurement for major contractors (EPC) and battery technology providers. This project will require significant FAT and QAQC for battery components and particularly for the HV grid connection equipment, including transformers, circuit breakers, and substation switchgear.

FFI is progressing plans for a large-scale green hydrogen production facility powered by dedicated renewable energy (likely solar PV). While early stage, this project will involve significant future procurement for electrolysers, solar equipment, and importantly, HV electrical infrastructure including substations, transformers, and switchgear for power supply and grid connection.

While the main pumped hydro facility is under construction, a new 275kV transmission line (Kidston Connection Project) is critical for its connection and is in active construction/commissioning. This involves ongoing QAQC and FAT for HV transmission components, substations, transformers, and switchgear as the project moves towards completion in 2025.

Critical 500kV transmission line linking Snowy 2.0 and new renewables to the grid. Procurement for major packages, including towers, conductors, and substation upgrades (HV transformers, circuit breakers, GIS), is ongoing. Specialist FAT and QAQC services for these components are highly relevant and actively sought for this multi-year project.

A critical new ~500kV interconnector linking the states. The project is in the advanced planning and approvals stage, with major procurement for transmission lines and associated HV substations (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) expected to commence in the next 18-24 months. Route selection and environmental assessments are ongoing.

projects.location: Wagga Wagga, NSW to Robertstown, SA
projects.summary: New 330kV transmission line and associated substations connecting NSW and SA. Construction is well advanced but ongoing, with critical HV substation equipment (transformers, GIS, switchgear) installation, testing, and commissioning activities still to be completed across multiple sites. Opportunities for specialist QAQC and FAT remain throughout 2024-2025.

Eastern section of Project EnergyConnect. Construction is ongoing, involving significant new 330kV transmission lines and upgrades to substations. Specialist sub-packages for HV equipment, including transformers, circuit breakers, and GIS, require rigorous FAT and site-based QAQC through the final construction and commissioning phases. Expected completion 2026.

A long-term vision to expand Tasmania's renewable generation and storage capacity. Includes new pumped hydro developments and significant upgrades/expansions to Tasmania's HV transmission network. While largely conceptual, early feasibility and detailed design stages are commencing, indicating future procurement for major HV infrastructure (transformers, switchgear, lines) will be substantial.

A 500MW/2-hour BESS planned for the Hunter region. Snowy Hydro announced early works for the BESS in December 2023, with procurement for major EPC and battery packages currently active or imminent. This project will require robust QAQC and FAT for the battery modules, inverters, and critically, the HV grid connection substation (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS).

Development of major transmission infrastructure within the New England REZ. This includes new transmission lines and a large central substation (Supernode) to connect multiple renewable projects. Early planning is underway, with major HV equipment procurement (transformers, GIS, circuit breakers) expected to ramp up in the coming years.

BNDES announced financing for this project in August 2023, with construction underway. While large equipment is ordered, specialist QAQC and FAT for HV step-up transformers, inverters, and substation switchgear during installation and commissioning phases are likely ongoing or imminent as the project progresses towards completion in 2025.

projects.projectName: Brazil – Serra das Vacas Wind Farm
projects.summary: Voltalia initiated construction in Q3 2023. This project involves the installation of wind turbines and associated electrical infrastructure. Specialist sub-packages for QAQC, FAT of turbine step-up transformers, substation HV transformers, circuit breakers, and grid connection switchgear will be crucial as the project moves towards energisation in 2025.

projects.projectName: Brazil – Rio do Vento Wind and Solar Complex (Expansion)
projects.summary: Casa dos Ventos announced a R$6 billion expansion in February 2024, adding 1GW of wind and solar capacity to the existing complex. This large-scale expansion will necessitate significant procurement for new turbines, solar panels, inverters, and substantial upgrades or new HV substation equipment (transformers, switchgear) for grid integration. Tenders for major packages expected 2025.

projects.projectName: Brazil – Sol do Sertão III Solar Farm
projects.summary: ANEEL authorized this project in January 2024. This 260MW solar farm is moving from authorization to detailed planning. Procurement for major components, including solar panels, inverters, and the HV substation (transformers, circuit breakers, switchgear) for grid connection, is anticipated to start within the next 12-18 months.

projects.projectName: Brazil – Lagoa do Mato III Wind Farm
projects.summary: ANEEL authorized construction in March 2024. This 110MW wind farm is now in advanced planning. Procurement for wind turbines, associated electrical infrastructure, and importantly, the HV substation components (transformers, circuit breakers, switchgear) for grid connection, is expected to begin in the next 12 months.

projects.projectName: Brazil – Morro do Chapéu Sul II Wind Farm
projects.summary: ANEEL approved this 130MW wind farm in February 2024. As planning progresses, procurement for wind turbines and critical HV components, including step-up transformers at the base of each turbine and the main substation's transformers and switchgear for grid integration, will be a key phase in 2025.

ANEEL authorized this project in March 2024. While smaller, it's a significant development for Roraima. Procurement for solar panels, inverters, and particularly the HV substation equipment (transformers, circuit breakers, switchgear) necessary for connecting to the isolated Roraima grid, will be critical within the next 12-18 months.

Construction for this 280MW solar complex began in Q4 2023. As civil works progress, specialized procurement and services for HV infrastructure (transformers, switchgear, GIS) at the grid connection point and for the internal solar farm substation will be active throughout 2024 and into 2025, leading up to commissioning.

Rio Energy announced plans for this new 190MW wind farm in early 2024. Following initial studies and permitting, procurement for turbines, their step-up transformers, and the main HV substation (including switchgear and circuit breakers) for grid connection will be a major upcoming phase, likely starting in 2025.
